The red blocks from the white shape seem to have merged with the green shape's red blocks. Let me check the current state more carefully.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state after overlap
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
for n in nodes:
    b = n['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in b]
    cols = [p[1] for p in b]
    if n['color'] in ['W', 'N', 'R']:
        print(f"  id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} rows[{min(rows)}-{max(rows)}] cols[{min(cols)}-{max(cols)}]")

print("\nAdjacency:")
for pair in seg['adjacency_list']:
    i, j = pair
    ci = next(n['color'] for n in nodes if n['id'] == i)
    cj = next(n['color'] for n in nodes if n['id'] == j)
    if ci != 'S' or cj != 'S':
        print(f"  {i}({ci}) - {j}({cj})")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
